description: Per-rule latency budgets. Closes the §3 'Per-rule p95 budget enforcement' top-priority gap in distribution/EVALUATION_METRICS.md. Source of latency data: benchmark-results/ilb-flagship-scorecard.md (or its JSON sibling at benchmarks/results/ilb-flagship/<date>.json). The CI gate at scripts/check-per-rule-budget.ts loads the latest flagship results and compares each rule's p95 against the budget here. Going over budget fails CI.

policy.note: p50 ≤ 50ms keeps the editor-on-save UX. p95 ≤ 250ms keeps pre-commit feasible on a 100-file staged set. p95 hard ceiling 1000ms means no single rule can blow the whole CI lint budget on a pathological input. Tolerance: 10% slack before failing (a 11% regression is the trigger). All numbers are wall-clock on a single rule, single fixture; ILB-Flagship is the canonical bench.

import-next/no-cycle.note: Cross-file traversal; tier=deep. Higher budget by design. See competitor_study_2026-05-09 — our DFS-with-cache rewrite halved the latency vs eslint-plugin-import.

secure-coding/no-hardcoded-credentials.note: Context-tier classifier; tier=fast. See competitor_study_2026-05-09 fix 3.

secure-coding/no-redos-vulnerable-regex.note: NFA analyzer via scslre; tier=fast. p95 reflects the 1% of regexes with high state-space explosion.

react-features/hooks-exhaustive-deps.note: Cross-component scope walk; tier=fast (no type info). See competitor_study_2026-05-09 fix 1 — non-array-deps reporting added.

process.addRule: Add the rule to `rules` with sensible p50 / p95 from the latest ILB-Flagship run. p50 ≤ p50_default_ms and p95 ≤ p95_default_ms unless documented in the rule's `note`.
process.increaseBudget: Allowed only if (a) the rule's correctness genuinely improved (write a note pointing at the change), or (b) the increase has been approved in PR review with a justification.
process.ciGate: `npm run check:per-rule-budget` runs the gate. Loads the latest ILB-Flagship results, joins on rule id, asserts each rule's measured p50/p95 ≤ budget × (1 + tolerance_pct/100).
